summary: Dashboard violates brand palette consistency rule by using generic gray/neutral UI instead of diner palette. The authenticated interface should maintain visual continuity with public surfaces using teal backgrounds, cream cards, and branded styling.

violations.description: Dashboard uses generic dark sidebar (#1f1b18 approximate) with neutral backgrounds instead of maintaining diner palette consistency with public surfaces. Should use teal (#9fd8d2) or cream (#fff8ea) primary surfaces.

Primary canvas uses neutral gray/beige backgrounds instead of required diner palette surfaces (teal #9fd8d2 or cream #fff8ea). Cards appear to use generic whites rather than cream variants from declared palette.
Signed-in app chrome has visually separated from public marketing palette, creating brand discontinuity. The two style systems should not bleed into separate treatments.

actions: Update dashboard primary background to use diner teal (#9fd8d2) or cream (#fff8ea) instead of neutral tones
Replace generic sidebar styling with diner palette treatment maintaining brand consistency
Ensure card surfaces use cream (#fff8ea) or white variants from declared palette rather than generic neutral backgrounds
Verify all authenticated UI surfaces maintain visual continuity with public brand treatment
